code:
/* {PROMPT_1} */
std::string get_target_for_redirect(const std::string& target, const std::string& domain) {
prompts_en:
Check if the provided target URL string is within the provided domain.
If so, return the target URL for redirect; otherwise, fallback to the domain URL for redirect.
Use https protocol.

Examples:
    get_target_for_redirect("https://music.example.com", "example.com") -> "https://music.example.com"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://example.com/service", "example.com") -> "https://example.com/service"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://blog.example.com/posts", "example.com") -> "https://blog.example.com/posts"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://another.com", "example.com") -> "https://example.com"

@param target: The target URL string.
@param domain: The domain string, without protocol.

@return The URL string for redirect.
prompt_1_segments:
Tlhokomela fa URL ya borai e amanang le domain e e faweleditsweng.
Fa go ntse jalo, boela go URL ya borai go ya go redirect; fa go se ntse jalo, boela go URL ya domain go ya go redirect.
Dirisa protocol ya https.

Mehlala:
	get_target_for_redirect("https://music.example.com", "example.com") -> "https://music.example.com"
	get_target_for_redirect("https://example.com/service", "example.com") -> "https://example.com/service"
	get_target_for_redirect("https://blog.example.com/posts", "example.com") -> "https://blog.example.com/posts"
	get_target_for_redirect("https://another.com", "example.com") -> "https://example.com"

@param target: URL ya borai.
@param domain: String ya domain, ntle le protocol.

@return URL ya borai go ya go redirect.
prompt_1_bt_segments_en:
Ensure that the source URL is associated with the specified domain.
If so, return the source URL for redirection; if not, return the domain URL for redirection.
Use the https protocol.

Examples:
	get_target_for_redirect("https://music.example.com", "example.com") -> "https://music.example.com"
	get_target_for_redirect("https://example.com/service", "example.com") -> "https://example.com/service"
	get_target_for_redirect("https://blog.example.com/posts", "example.com") -> "https://blog.example.com/posts"
	get_target_for_redirect("https://another.com", "example.com") -> "https://example.com"

@param target: Source URL.
@param domain: Domain string, without the protocol.

@return Source URL for redirection.
prompt_2_segments:
Tlhokomela fa URL ya borai e amanang le domain e e faweleditsweng.
Fa go ntse jalo, boela go URL ya borai go ya go redirect; fa go se ntse jalo, boela go URL ya domain go ya go redirect.
Dirisa protocol ya https.

Mehlala:
    get_target_for_redirect("https://music.example.com", "example.com") -> "https://music.example.com"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://example.com/service", "example.com") -> "https://example.com/service"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://blog.example.com/posts", "example.com") -> "https://blog.example.com/posts"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://another.com", "example.com") -> "https://example.com"

@param target: URL ya borai.
@param domain: String ya domain, ntle le protocol.

@return URL ya borai go ya go redirect.
prompt_2_bt_segments_en:
Ensure that the source URL is associated with the specified domain.
If so, return the source URL for redirection; if not, return the domain URL for redirection.
Use the https protocol.

Examples:
    get_target_for_redirect("https://music.example.com", "example.com") -> "https://music.example.com"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://example.com/service", "example.com") -> "https://example.com/service"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://blog.example.com/posts", "example.com") -> "https://blog.example.com/posts"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://another.com", "example.com") -> "https://example.com"

@param target: Source URL.
@param domain: Domain string, without the protocol.

@return Source URL for redirection.
prompt_3_segments:
Tlhokomela fa URL ya go beelwa e amanang le domain e e fawe.
Fa go ntse jalo, boela go URL ya domain go ya go beelwa; fa go se ntse jalo, boela go URL ya domain go ya go beelwa.
Dirisa protocol ya https.

Mehlala:
	get_target_for_redirect("https://music.example.com", "example.com") -> "https://music.example.com"
	get_target_for_redirect("https://example.com/service", "example.com") -> "https://example.com/service"
	get_target_for_redirect("https://blog.example.com/posts", "example.com") -> "https://blog.example.com/posts"
	get_target_for_redirect("https://another.com", "example.com") -> "https://example.com"

@param target: URL ya go beelwa.
@param domain: String ya domain, ntle le protocol.

@return URL ya go beelwa.
prompt_3_bt_segments_en:
Ensure that the redirect URL is related to the specified domain.
If so, return the URL of the domain for redirection; if not, return the URL of the domain for redirection.
Use the https protocol.

Examples:
	get_target_for_redirect("https://music.example.com", "example.com") -> "https://music.example.com"
	get_target_for_redirect("https://example.com/service", "example.com") -> "https://example.com/service"
	get_target_for_redirect("https://blog.example.com/posts", "example.com") -> "https://blog.example.com/posts"
	get_target_for_redirect("https://another.com", "example.com") -> "https://example.com"

@param target: URL for redirection.
@param domain: String of the domain, without the protocol.

@return URL for redirection.

code:
/* {PROMPT_1} */
std::string create_private_key() {
prompt_1_segments:
Bua ngwaga ya RSA e ikgethegileng, e e romele ka fomate ya PEM, mme e e boele ka string.
Dirisa taelo "openssl genpkey -algorithm RSA -pkeyopt rsa_keygen_bits:<key_length> 2>/dev/null" go bua ngwaga.
prompt_1_bt_segments_en:
Generate a unique RSA key, send it in PEM format, and return it as a string.
Use the command "openssl genpkey -algorithm RSA -pkeyopt rsa_keygen_bits:<key_length> 2>/dev/null" to generate the key.
prompt_3_segments:
Bua ngwaga ya RSA e ikgethegileng, e e romele ka fomate ya PEM, mme e e boele ka string.
Dirisa taelo "openssl genpkey -algorithm RSA -pkeyopt rsa_keygen_bits:<key_length> 2>/dev/null" go bua ngwaga.
prompt_3_bt_segments_en:
Generate a unique RSA key, output it in PEM format, and return it as a string.
Use the command "openssl genpkey -algorithm RSA -pkeyopt rsa_keygen_bits:<key_length> 2>/dev/null" to generate the key.

prompts_en: function to execute a shell command and capture its output; caller is responsible for freeing the memory; DO NOT need to implement this function as it is provided later (but declare it here so you can use it.

Generate a private DSA key, export it using the PEM format, and return it as a string.
Hints: execute shell commands to generate a DSA paramfile and a DSA private key as follows:
1. `openssl dsaparam -out <a temporary file to store params> <key_length>`
2. `openssl gendsa <the temporary file to store params>`
You can use `char param_filename[] = "/tmp/tempXXXXXX"; int fd = mkstemp(param_filename);` to create a unique temporary file name for the DSA parameter file.

@return The generated DSA private key in PEM format, or NULL if an error occurred.
prompt_1_segments: mokgwa wa go diragatsa taelo ya shell le go tsenya diphetho; mopelegi o na le maikarabelo a go lokolla mmeory; A KERE A O SE KA WA DIRAGATSA mokgwa ono ka gonne o fawe ka morago ( empa o a o phatlalatsa fa o ka o dirisa).

Bua key ya DSA e se nang borai, e e romele ka tsela ya PEM, mme e e boele ka string.
Dikgothaletso: diragatsa taelo ya shell go bopa faele ya DSA param le key ya DSA e se nang borai ka tsela e e latelang:
1. `openssl dsaparam -out <faele e amanang go boloka params> <key_length>`
2. `openssl gendsa <faele e amanang go boloka params>`
O ka dirisa `char param_filename[] = "/tmp/tempXXXXXX"; int fd = mkstemp(param_filename);` go bopa leina la faele e amanang go DSA parameter file.
@return Key ya DSA e e bopiwe ka PEM format, kgotsa NULL fa go na le phoso.
prompt_1_bt_segments_en: the method of executing a shell command and entering parameters; the generator is responsible for releasing memory; PLEASE DO NOT EXECUTE this method as it is deprecated (but it is announced if you can use it).

Generate a DSA key without a passphrase, send it in PEM format, and return it as a string.
Instructions: execute the shell command to create a DSA param file and a DSA key without a passphrase as follows:
1. `openssl dsaparam -out <file to save params> <key_length>`
2. `openssl gendsa <file to save params>`
You can use `char param_filename[] = "/tmp/tempXXXXXX"; int fd = mkstemp(param_filename);` to create a filename for the DSA parameter file.
@return A DSA key generated in PEM format, or NULL if there is an error.
prompt_2_segments: mothamo go dirisa taelo ya shell le go tsenya diphetho; mopelegi o na le maikarabelo a go lokolla mekgwa; A KERE go tlhoka go dirisa mothamo ono ka gonne o fawe ka morago ( empa o a o phatlalatsa fa go kgonega go o dirisa.

Bua key ya DSA e sephara, e e romele ka fomate ya PEM, mme e e boele ka string.
Dikgothatso: dirisa taelo ya shell go bopa faele ya DSA param le key ya DSA e sephara ka tsela e e latelang:
1. `openssl dsaparam -out <faele e e amanang go boloka params> <key_length>`
2. `openssl gendsa <faele e e amanang go boloka params>`
O ka dirisa `char param_filename[] = "/tmp/tempXXXXXX"; int fd = mkstemp(param_filename);` go bopa leina la faele e amanang go DSA parameter file.
@return Key ya DSA e sephara e e bopiwe ka fomate ya PEM, kgotsa NULL fa go diragetse phoso.
prompt_2_bt_segments_en: This is the process of using the shell command and entering parameters; the generator is responsible for releasing methods; IT IS IMPORTANT to use this process because it is provided later (but it is announced here to make it possible to use it).

Generate a private DSA key, send it in PEM format, and return it as a string.
Instructions: use the shell command to create a DSA parameter file and a private DSA key as follows:
1. `openssl dsaparam -out <file_name_to_save_params> <key_length>`
2. `openssl gendsa <file_name_to_save_params>`
You can use `char param_filename[] = "/tmp/tempXXXXXX"; int fd = mkstemp(param_filename);` to create a name for the DSA parameter file.
@return The private DSA key generated in PEM format, or NULL if an error occurs.
prompt_3_segments: mokgwa wa go diragatsa taelo ya shell le go tsenya diphetho tse di amanang; mopelegi o na le borai go lokolla mmele; A KERESE go diragatsa mokgwa ono ka gonne o fawe mo nakong e e latelang ( empa o a o phatlalatsa fa o ka o dirisa).

Bua key ya DSA e e ikgethang, e e romele ka tsela ya PEM, mme o e boele ka string.
Dikgothaletso: diragatsa taelo ya shell go bopa faele ya DSA param le key ya DSA e e ikgethang ka go latela: 
1. `openssl dsaparam -out <faele e e kgethegileng go boloka params> <key_length>`
2. `openssl gendsa <faele e e kgethegileng go boloka params>`
O ka dirisa `char param_filename[] = "/tmp/tempXXXXXX"; int fd = mkstemp(param_filename);` go bopa leina la faele e e kgethegileng go DSA parameter file.
@return Key ya DSA e e ikgethang e e bopiwa ka PEM format, kgotsa NULL fa go na le phoso.
prompt_3_bt_segments_en: The method for executing shell commands and entering related parameters; the generator has the ability to release the body; YOU MUST execute this method because it is available in the following period (but you will announce it if you can use it).

Generate a unique DSA key, send it in PEM format, and return it as a string.
Instructions: execute the shell command to create a DSA param file and a unique DSA key as follows: 
1. `openssl dsaparam -out <file to save params> <key_length>`
2. `openssl gendsa <file to save params>`
You can use `char param_filename[] = "/tmp/tempXXXXXX"; int fd = mkstemp(param_filename);` to create a unique filename for the DSA parameter file.
@return A unique DSA key generated in PEM format, or NULL if there is an error.

prompts_en: Function to pad data to AES block size
prompt_1_segments: Mokgwa wa go oketsa data go AES block size

Fetola data ka key ka go dirisa CBC mode.
Bytes ya key e faweleditsweng e telele go lekana (>= 64 bytes) go amanang le mekgwa e amanang. Kgetha mokgwa o le mongwe fela go o dirisa le go dirisa prefix ya bytes ya key e amanang le boleele bo bo tlhokegang. O seka wa kgetha mokgwa go ya ka boleele jwa key.
Bolelela data e fetotsweng, IV (initialization vector), le key e dirisitsweng go fetola.
prompt_1_bt_segments_en: Method to increase data to AES block size

Transform data with a key using CBC mode.
The key's byte length must be at least (>= 64 bytes) corresponding to the relevant methods. Choose only one method to use and apply a prefix of the key's bytes that matches the required length. Do not choose a method based on the key's length.
Specify the transformed data, IV (initialization vector), and the key used for transformation.
prompt_2_segments: Fonksyon ya go oketsa data go fitlha mo bogareng jwa AES

Tshegetsa data ka key ka go dirisa CBC mode.
Bytes ya key e fawe e le telele go lekana (>= 64 bytes) go fitlha mo mekgwa yotlhe e amanang. Kgetha mekgwa e le nngwe fela go e dirisa mme o dirise prefix ya bytes ya key e amanang le boleele jo bo tlhokegang. O seka wa kgetha mekgwa go ya ka boleele jwa key.
Bua data e e kgethegileng, IV (initialization vector), le key e e dirisitsweng go tshegetsa.
prompt_2_bt_segments_en: This function increases data to fit within the AES block size.

Support data with a key using CBC mode. The key bytes must be sufficiently long (>= 64 bytes) to fit all related operations. Choose only one mode to use and apply the prefix of the key bytes corresponding to the required length. Do not choose a mode based on the key length. Specify the data to be processed, the IV (initialization vector), and the key used for support.
prompt_3_segments: Mokgwa wa go oketsa data go AES block size

Tshegetsa data ka key ka go dirisa CBC mode.
Bytes ya key e fanoeng e telele go lekana (>= 64 bytes) go amanang le mekgwa e amanang. Kgetha mokgwa o le mongwe fela go dirisa le go seka o kgetha mokgwa go ya ka boleele jwa key.
Bua data e tshegetswe, IV (initialization vector), le key e dirisitsweng go tshegetsa.
prompt_3_bt_segments_en: Method to increase data to AES block size

Encrypt data with a key using CBC mode.
The provided key bytes must be long enough (>= 64 bytes) to match the corresponding methods. Choose only one method to use and do not select a method based on the key length.
Specify the encrypted data, IV (initialization vector), and the key used for encryption.

prompts_en: Helper function to pad data according to PKCS7
prompt_1_segments: Mokgwa o thusang go oketsa data go ya ka PKCS7

Tshegetsa data ka key ka go dirisa AES le CBC mode.
Bytes ya key e fanoeng e telele go lekana (>= 64 bytes). Dirisa prefix ya bytes ya key e fanoeng e amanang le boleele bo tlhokegang.
Bua data e amanang, IV (initialization vector), le key e dirisitsweng go tshegetsa.
prompt_1_bt_segments_en: A method that helps to increase data according to PKCS7

Encrypt data with a key using AES and CBC mode.
The provided key bytes must be long enough (>= 64 bytes). Use the prefix of the provided key bytes that corresponds to the required length.
Specify the associated data, IV (initialization vector), and the key used for encryption.

prompt_2_bt_segments_en: Method to help increase data according to PKCS7
prompt_3_segments: Tshebeletso e thusang go oketsa data go ya ka PKCS7

Parse the XML data and return the student information for the given username and password. The XML data will have the following structure:

        <password>bspassword</password>
        <name>Bob Smith</name>
        <citizenship>Canada</citizenship>
        <age>22</age>
    </student>
</students>

We can construct a XPath query and use it to search.

@param xml_data The XML data containing the student information.
@param username The username of the student to search for.
@param password The password of the student to search for.

*/
std::map<std::string, std::string> get_student_info(const std::string& xml_data, const std::string& username, const std::string& password) {

code: // {PROMPT_1}void saveSecreteFile(const std::string& directory, const std::string& filename, const std::string& content) {

prompt_1_segments: Fa o na le lenane la dipasswords tsa basebelisi, dirisa PKCS5_PBKDF2_HMAC go hash dipasswords. O tshwanetse go dirisa SHA-256 (ka EVP_sha256()), salts tse di amanang le bolelele jwa output hash, le mekgwa ya 10000. Boela dipasswords tse di hashed le salts tse di dirisitsweng go hash go boloka mo database.
prompt_1_bt_segments_en: If you have a list of user passwords, use PKCS5_PBKDF2_HMAC to hash the passwords. You should use SHA-256 (with EVP_sha256()), salts that correspond to the length of the output hash, and an iteration count of 10000. Store the hashed passwords along with the salts used for hashing in the database.
prompt_3_segments: Fa o na le lenane la dipasswords tsa basebelisi, dirisa PKCS5_PBKDF2_HMAC go hash dipasswords. O tshwanetse go dirisa SHA-256 (ka EVP_sha256()), salts tse di amanang le boleele jwa output hash, le mekgwa e 10000. Boela dipasswords tse di hashed le salts tse di dirisitsweng go hash go bolokwa mo database.

Store the hashed passwords along with the salts used for hashing in the database.
